title: Peak-commit decode: β-sharpened softmax replaces mean-of-map soft-argmax
hypothesis: After exps 10-12 (all kept: layout head, pretrained trunk, gated dark expert; 2489 -> 1857 m) the binding constraint is no longer feature quality but the decode itself: global soft-argmax returns the probability-weighted MEAN of the whole 32x32 field, so any residual diffuse or multimodal mass drags every prediction toward the map centroid. Three lines of evidence converge on this. (1) The error profile has the exact shrinkage signature: medians sit at 1.0-1.9 km against a ~3.2 km center-guess floor with mean/median ratios of only ~1.15-1.25 in every area x bucket cell — a light-tailed, globally-shrunk distribution, not the heavy tail that confident-but-wrong mode commitment would produce. Under a mixture model 'p mass near truth + (1-p) diffuse', median ≈ (1-p) x center-distance gives p ≈ 0.45-0.6 across buckets: the field already places roughly half its mass near the right answer and the expectation decode throws that away. (2) The training loss actively teaches this failure: the coordinate term is an L2 on the decoded expectation, whose minimizer IS the posterior mean, while the §6 metric is a MEDIAN of radial error, which rewards committing to the dominant mode — a direct loss-metric mismatch nothing in the history has addressed. (3) The only prior attack on the decode, exp 3 (argmax-anchored 5x5-window soft-argmax), was refuted in the from-scratch-encoder era, and its own conclusion attributed the failure to near-uniform fields making the argmax pure noise; that failure mode no longer applies now that exps 10-12 produced fields with real structure (first-ever per-lighting-bucket error gradient). The materially new angle vs exp 3: no hard argmax anchor and no window — a fully differentiable global sharpening (decode from softmax(β·logits), inverse-temperature β = 3) that leaves a uniform field exactly uniform (so the untrained model still decodes to the map center — same bounded-downside floor as every kept experiment) but drives any field with a dominant mode toward that mode, and lets the existing coordinate L2 automatically retrain the network on the committed answer.
method: One line in model/model.py's TinyLocNet.forward(): the decode distribution becomes p = softmax(DECODE_BETA * logits) with a new module constant DECODE_BETA = 3.0; the soft-argmax expectation over cell centers, the confidence head, and the gated two-head field are untouched. loss_fn is left byte-identical: its cross-entropy consumes the raw logits (log_softmax(logits)), so the field keeps learning the same calibrated Gaussian-bump targets, while its coordinate L2 reads pred[:, :2] and therefore automatically grades the sharpened, committed decode — training and inference stay coupled on exactly what flies. No new parameters, train.py unchanged, ONNX contract and deployment gates unaffected.
expected_outcome: If the shrinkage arithmetic is right (~half the field mass already near truth), commitment converts hedged 1.3-1.9 km medians into mode-position errors: predicted worst-case median 900-1600 m (from 1856.69 m), improving all buckets, largest absolute gains where fields are most peaked (bright buckets). A confirming secondary signature: mean/median ratio should RISE (occasional confident misses replace universal hedging) even as the median falls. If instead the field's dominant modes are usually wrong, the median regresses toward 2-3 km (exp 3's fate, 2937 m) — which would cleanly refute the shrinkage reading and redirect the loop at field quality (training-scale, relighting-side signal) rather than decode mechanics.
init_strategy: pretrained:mobilenet_v3_small IMAGENET1K_V1 features[0..8] (torchvision, BSD-3 — unchanged from exp 11)
eli5: The model draws a heat map of 'where might this photo be?' over the whole city, then has to give one answer. Until now it answered with the average of every warm spot at once — like aiming a dart at the average of three boards you suspect, which lands you in the wall between them. We now turn up the contrast on the heat map first, so the strongest hotspot dominates and the faint background noise vanishes, and the model answers at that hotspot. It is also graded during training on this committed answer, so it learns to put its hotspot in the right place rather than hedging toward the middle of the map.

stages.name: Camera frame
stages.detail: 128×128 px aerial crop, one of 6 simulated lighting conditions

stages.name: Feature extractor
stages.detail: ImageNet-pretrained MobileNetV3-Small (first 9 blocks, 190k params), gently fine-tuned

stages.name: Layout summary
stages.detail: a slim extra layer condenses the 8×8 feature grid into a 512-number code that remembers what sits where in the crop; the texture average (48 numbers) is kept alongside

stages.name: Lighting gate
stages.detail: a tiny network reads the crop's own average brightness together with the 48-number texture summary and outputs one number: how night-like is this shot?

stages.name: Probability map
stages.detail: two scorers read the crop — the layout-aware scorer and the brightness-only low-light scorer — and the gate blends their per-cell guesses into one heat map

stages.name: Decode
stages.detail: the heat map's contrast is turned up first (peaks boosted, faint background suppressed), then the answer is the balance point of the sharpened map — committing to the strongest hotspot instead of averaging every faint guess across the whole city

stages.name: Confidence
stages.detail: separate 0–1 score; low means the model abstains rather than guessing

stages.name: Training signal
stages.detail: the position-error penalty is now measured on the committed, sharpened answer — the model is graded on exactly the answer it would fly with, while the heat map itself still learns from the same hotter/colder per-cell signal as before

implementation_brief:
Edit model/model.py ONLY; model/train.py needs no changes (its param groups, LRs, data loading, and CLI are untouched by this experiment).

1) In model/model.py, add one module-level constant directly below TARGET_SIGMA_CELLS:

    DECODE_BETA = 3.0  # inverse-temperature sharpening of the decode distribution (exp 14): softmax(β·logits) commits the soft-argmax to the dominant mode instead of the field mean; a uniform field stays uniform, so the untrained decode still starts at the map center

2) In TinyLocNet.forward(), change exactly one line. Replace:

    p = torch.softmax(logits, dim=1)

with:

    p = torch.softmax(DECODE_BETA * logits, dim=1)

Everything else in forward() stays byte-identical: lum, fmap, f, layout, bright_logits, dark_logits, gate g, the blended logits, the soft-argmax expectation u/v over cell_u/cell_v, conf, the concatenated output, and the (out, logits) return when return_logits=True. Note the returned logits remain the RAW blended logits (pre-sharpening) — that is load-bearing.

3) Leave loss_fn ENTIRELY unchanged. Its cross-entropy consumes the raw logits via log_softmax(logits), so the field keeps learning calibrated Gaussian-bump targets at temperature 1; do NOT apply DECODE_BETA there. Its coordinate L2 reads pred[:, :2], which now flows through the sharpened decode automatically — that retargeting is the intended training effect, not a bug. The conf BCE term is likewise unchanged.

4) Append a short 'Exp 14' paragraph to the module docstring (keeping the Exp 10/11/12 history paragraphs) explaining: expected-value soft-argmax over the full field returns the posterior MEAN, so diffuse/multimodal mass shrinks every prediction toward the map centroid (medians 1.0-1.9 km vs the ~3.2 km center floor with mean/median ≈ 1.2 — the shrinkage signature); decoding from softmax(DECODE_BETA·logits) commits to the dominant mode while a uniform field stays uniform (bounded downside at init), and the coordinate L2 now grades the committed decode.

5) Touch nothing else. Explicitly unchanged: GRID_K, TARGET_SIGMA_CELLS, _grid_centers, _build_pretrained_trunk and the strict load from model/pretrained/mnv3s_features8.pt, the ImageNet normalization buffers, layout_squeeze, loc_logits, dark_logits, gate, conf_head, build_model, loss_fn, export_onnx (opset 17, dynamo=False, input name 'frame', output name 'uvc'), and estimate_position.

Contracts to preserve (frozen scorer depends on them): model/train.py CLI must remain `python -m model.train --area <name> --out-dir <dir> [--data-dir D] [--epochs N] [--max-crops-per-bucket N] [--seed N]` and export one ONNX per area at <out-dir>/models/<area>.onnx taking a 1x3x128x128 float32 input in [0,1] and returning [[u, v, conf]] with u, v normalized map coords and conf in [0,1]. Deployment gates: exported ONNX ≤ 4 MiB and host latency proxy ≤ 250 ms — this change adds zero parameters and one scalar multiply, so both gates are unaffected (current export 3.29 MB, 0.77 ms).

Sanity property the implementer can check mentally: softmax of a constant vector is uniform for any β, so an untrained model still decodes near (0.5, 0.5) — the same bounded-downside floor every prior experiment relied on.
